code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Multi-purchase defects overriding initial positive experience
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Explicit warnings against purchase with defect evidence
    - Safety hazards confirmed through repeated use

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Requires pre-processing for full functionality
    - Lingering functional drawbacks post-resolution
    - Assembly difficulties impacting initial experience

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise effectiveness praise without negatives →5
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Close comparisons to premium brands with satisfaction
    - 3+ praised aspects (functionality/design/durability)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Practical benefits dramatically outweigh limitations
    - Long-term validation with sustained performance

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if satisfaction expressed
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Functional praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Functional praise + single functional flaw →4
    - Brief praise without context →5 if core needs fully met
    - Material maintenance needs →5 if non-functional
    - Assembly difficulties →5 if outcome is flawless
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Premium brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Works after washing but edges stay stiff"
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "First lasted 3 years, second failed in 2 months"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase defects override initial success →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Vibrant colors need ironing but worth it for durability"
<Reasoning>Non-functional maintenance vs core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Good for light use; material concerns for heavy loads"
<Reasoning>Contextual limitation with compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Constant adjustments needed for basic function"
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Sharper image than expected. Menu buttons confuse occasionally"
<Reasoning>Functional excellence outweighs non-functional issue →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Assembly nightmare but now withstands heavy use"
<Reasoning>Post-setup functionality excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>

  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (33 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 6
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 5 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 1, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I can forgive the fact that there was blood on one of the math mats when I opened the package.  Things happen and I'm sorry that someone was injured.  What I can't get past is the fact that there are so many snags.  It's like these two mats were seco..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 33 errors, 13 borderline out of 80 examples."}
